methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $41M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
First Resonance is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $40.8M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 9 private and 1 public company operating in the same sector and at the same or adjacent funding stage, including peers such as 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), and Collaborative Robotics ($500M, Series B).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression methodology uses the $41M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage progression, implying a valuation of $82M. Peer-median analysis across the 6-company robotics cohort yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The disparity reflects material heterogeneity in stage advancement and market positioning within the peer set.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $69M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $578M. The 17x spread between low and high estimates reflects significant uncertainty regarding First Resonance's path to subsequent funding rounds and competitive positioning relative to better-capitalized Series B peers. Stage discount relative to peer medians appears material; downside case assumes conservative multiple application to current capital base, while upside case approaches peer median valuations conditional on near-term traction or capital raise momentum.
